entries.source:
Chat Edit: '✅ Complete! All chat-to-engine wiring implemented and built successfully.

What was done:
1. Chat → AgenticEngine Wiring
Connected AgenticEngine::responseReady → addAssistantMessage + finishStreaming
Connected AgenticEngine::tokenGenerated for streaming progress tracking
Connected AgenticEngine::modelReady → setModelStatus for all chat panels
Added editor context gathering (selected text, file path, surrounding code) before sending messages
2. Model Lifecycle UI
Added setModelStatus(modelName, ready) method to show "✓ Model: <name>" or "⚠ Model: Not loaded"
Status badge changes color (green when ready, red when not loaded)
Input field automatically disabled until model loads
Placeholder text changes: "Ask AI anything..." when ready, "Load a model to start chatting..." when not
All chat tabs get notified when model loads/unloads
3. Streaming Behavior
addAssistantMessage("", true) starts streaming with "Generating…" placeholder
updateStreamingMessage(partial) updates the streaming text block in real-time
finishStreaming() clears streaming state and finalizes the message
Added addSystemMessage(msg) for status/error messages in gray italic
4. Context Injection
onChatMessageSent now gathers:
Selected code (code blocks)
Current file path + line number
Full file content (if < 5000 chars)
All context passed to AgenticEngine::processMessage(message, editorContext)
Test it:
Load a GGUF model via AI → Load Model
Watch status change from "⚠ Model: Not loaded" → "✓ Model: <name>"
Input field enables automatically
Type a message → responses stream from local GGUF (no HTTP)
Select code in editor → it gets injected as context in your next chat message
Your IDE now behaves exactly like Cursor/Copilot but with pure local inference and zero network dependencies! 🚀



Finish ALL missing and non missing features and even check on C:desktop onlydrive for the pure masm ide for anything that might be more useful as i wanted to basically mix the two together at this point to see if anything would give it the edge '
